title: Racosperma calligerum
taxonomy: Racosperma calligerum is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is commonly known as the golden parrot bean or golden bird's foot. The classification of Racosperma calligerum is as follows: it belongs to the subgenus Acacia, and its full classification is Acacia calligerum or more specifically, Vachellia calligerum. This classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a species within the genus Vachellia. The plant's classification has undergone changes over time due to ongoing research and reclassification of the Acacia genus.

intro: Racosperma calligerum, also known as Acacia calligerum, is a small tree or shrub native to Australia, belonging to the family Fabaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in traditional medicine to treat various ailments such as wounds and skin conditions.
